milestone.status: game-session token request body producer mapped
milestone.meaning: The R8 payload passed to the shared submitter at 0x14029B047 is a serialized JSON body containing an accountId field, not an opaque or unused body string.

headers.name: Authorization
headers.value_shape: Bearer + space + auth token string selected from global auth state

body.final_selector: capacity >= 0x10 selects heap pointer; smaller capacity keeps inline buffer

fields.name: accountId
fields.evidence: 0x140634760 receives RCX=[RSP+0x68], RDX=0x140B69850 ('accountId'), and R8=0x140B69859 (end/NUL-adjacent pointer for that literal).

serialization_evidence: 0x14029AFA3 calls 0x1405ACEC0 with RCX=[RBP-0x8] and RDX=[RBP-0x30], binding the builder/writer to the final body string slot.
0x14029AFE4 calls 0x1405ACC80 on the writer state at [RBP-0x8].
0x14029B01B calls 0x1407B61F4 immediately before the final small-string selector and submitter call.

submitter_arguments.rdx: endpoint string at [RBP-0x48] selected by bit7 small-string storage logic
submitter_arguments.r8: serialized JSON body string at [RBP-0x30] selected by capacity-threshold small-string storage logic

opennxt_947_implications: OpenNXT's 947-compatible auth/session bridge should expect a JSON POST-like request to game-session/v1/tokens.
The request should include bearer Authorization plus Content-Type and Accept set to application/json.
The body must carry accountId; the exact accountId source still needs a producer trace, but the client-side field name and body slot are now pinned down.
next_steps: Trace the accountId value producer before 0x14029AED4 so OpenNXT can map the client account/session source object.
Trace the auth token global around 0x140E58AD8/0x140E58AEF to link Authorization Bearer construction to the earlier directlogin/auth lifecycle.
Map response consumers for game-session/v1/tokens to identify the returned session token storage slot.
